title: Package beadflow as standalone CLI for any beads repo
description: Epic for packaging beadflow so it can be run in any beads-enabled repository. Running 'beadflow' should start a local web server serving the React frontend, automatically export beads data on startup, and watch the beads database for changes to refresh the frontend without manual reload. Goal: zero-config drop-in visualization for any project using bd.

title: Improve content display
description: Epic for improving how issue content is displayed in the UI — sidebar enhancements, navigation, and related context.

title: MVP
description: Minimum viable product epic tracking all top-level beadflow features.

title: Force simulation unstable with large graphs (40+ nodes)
description: When loading a project with many open issues (40+), nodes briefly appear then fly off screen. The force simulation spreads nodes too far and fitView cannot keep up. Need to tune simulation parameters (link distance, charge strength, bounds) to be stable at larger scales.
acceptance_criteria: Graph with 40+ visible nodes settles into a readable layout without nodes flying off screen.
close_reason: Charge/link scaled by node count, hard bounding force added, velocityDecay increased

title: Bundle frontend as self-contained static assets
description: Build the React frontend into static files that are bundled inside the npm package. The CLI serves these built assets from the package directory rather than requiring a separate build step in the user's repo.
close_reason: Frontend bundled via vite build, prepare script builds on npm install, dist/ gitignored

title: CLI entry point: beadflow command
description: Create a 'beadflow' CLI entry point (npm package bin). Running 'beadflow' in any beads-enabled repo should start the local web server. Package.json bin field, minimal argument parsing (--port, --host).
close_reason: CLI entry point bin/beadflow.js serves dist/ and runs bd export dynamically, prints URL on start

title: Watch beads DB and auto-refresh frontend
description: Watch the beads database file (or run periodic bd export) for changes. When a change is detected, re-export beads.json and notify the frontend via SSE or WebSocket so the graph refreshes automatically without manual reload.

title: Auto-export beads on server start
description: On startup, the CLI runs 'bd export' and writes beads.json into a temp or serve directory before starting the web server. Ensures fresh data is available immediately without manual export step.

title: Show related beads in sidebar
description: When a bead is selected, the sidebar should list related beads (dependencies and dependents) as clickable items. Clicking a related bead selects it in the graph and updates the sidebar.
close_reason: Related beads in sidebar are clickable, select+zoom to neighborhood in graph

title: Track selected bead in browser history
description: Reflect the currently selected bead ID in the URL (e.g. ?selected=beadflow-xyz) so that browser back/forward navigation restores the selection. Deep-linking to a URL with a selected param should pre-select that node on load.
close_reason: URL tracks focused bead, back/forward re-zooms, deep-linking supported, pan after focus handled via synthetic history entry

title: Highlight critical path
description: Identify and highlight the longest dependency chain (critical path) through the visible graph. Highlight those nodes and edges distinctly.
close_reason: Implemented with toolbar toggle, amber highlights on critical nodes and edges, BFS upstream for selected node, global longest path fallback

title: Render focused issue graph with expand on demand
description: Show a small initial set of nodes. Each node has expand buttons for incoming and outgoing dependency edges. Clicking expand loads and shows connected neighbors.

title: Pin dragged node during manual repositioning
description: When the user manually drags a node, the force simulation should continue running at low temperature (alpha) so other nodes gently adjust, but the dragged node itself must be pinned (fx/fy fixed) so it stays where the user placed it. On drag end, unpin the node and let the simulation cool naturally.

title: Server: stream bd export directly, wrapping JSONL into JSON array on the fly
description: Replace lib/exportBeads.js wrapper with inline streaming in bin/beadflow.js. The /beads.json endpoint should spawn 'bd export' as a child process, pipe its stdout, and wrap the JSONL lines into a JSON array response without intermediate parse/serialize round-trip.
close_reason: Replaced exportBeads() call with inline spawn of bd export, streaming JSONL lines wrapped into a JSON array. Dropped lib/exportBeads.js import.

title: Zoom to node centers selected node, not neighborhood
description: When zooming to a node (via sidebar click, double-click, or back navigation), the view should center on the selected node with its neighborhood visible around it. Currently fitView fits the entire neighborhood bounding box, which may leave the selected node off-center.
acceptance_criteria: Selected node is visually centered in the viewport after zoom. Neighbors are visible but may be partially clipped at edges.
close_reason: Node centered in canvas area with sidebar offset applied

title: Highlight epic nodes more prominently in graph
description: Epic-type issues should be visually distinct from tasks/features/bugs in the graph. Display epics with a larger card (wider and taller), thicker border, and a ◆ prefix on the title text. Keep the same color scheme as features. Scope: node appearance only, not edges.
acceptance_criteria: Epic nodes render larger than standard nodes. Border is visibly thicker. Title is prefixed with ◆. Non-epic nodes are unaffected.
close_reason: Epics render at 320×110px with thicker border, purple tint, rounded corners, and ◆ icon

title: Edge arrowhead overlaps the edge line
description: The arrowhead marker at the end of floating edges visually overlaps the edge line. The path end point and marker geometry need to be reconciled so the arrowhead sits cleanly at the tip without the line poking through it.

title: Toolbar with graph view controls
description: Add a toolbar to the canvas with three buttons: (1) 'Hide closed' — toggle visibility of all closed nodes; (2) 'Prune' — for the selected node, collapse to only show its direct 1st-level connections; (3) 'Show all' — expand all nodes in the graph into the visible set.

title: Focus button: center node and expand all 1st-level connections
description: When clicking the 'focus' button on a node, center that node in the viewport and automatically expand all its direct dependencies and dependents (1st level in both directions), replacing the current view.

title: Expand root to first open level by default
description: On initial load, automatically expand from the root node(s) to show all open issues on the next dependency level. Closed issues at that level should not be expanded further automatically. This gives a useful default view without overwhelming the graph.

title: Spring layout spreads independent clusters too far apart
description: When there are multiple disconnected clusters in the visible graph, the force simulation spreads them too far apart. Independent clusters should be arranged compactly within a circular area rather than in a line. Consider adding a cluster-aware bounding force or initializing cluster positions in a circular arrangement before simulation.

title: Shade down closed issues in graph
description: Visually de-emphasize closed nodes — lower opacity, greyed palette — so open work stands out.

title: Reload beads graph data
description: Add a reload button to re-run bd export and refresh the graph without a full page reload. Should re-fetch /beads.json or re-run the export script and update the React state.

title: Issue detail sidebar
description: Clicking a node opens a sidebar showing full issue details: title, status, priority, description, and dependency list.

title: Parse bd export JSONL into graph data
description: Read bd export output (JSONL) and convert issues + dependencies into React Flow nodes and edges.

title: Scaffold Vite + React + React Flow app
description: Set up the initial project: Vite, React, React Flow, basic layout with a canvas area and sidebar panel.

title: Label edges with dependency type
description: Show the dependency type (e.g. 'blocks') as a small label on each edge in the graph.

title: Node styling by status and priority
description: Color-code nodes by status (open/in_progress/closed) and show priority visually (badge or border weight).
